root_text.an9.45:1.2: Kittāvatā nu kho, āvuso, ubhatobhāgavimutto vutto bhagavatā”ti?
root_text.an9.45:2.1: “Idhāvuso, bhikkhu vivicceva kāmehi …pe… paṭhamaṁ jhānaṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an9.45:2.2: Yathā yathā ca tadāyatanaṁ tathā tathā naṁ kāyena phusitvā viharati, paññāya ca naṁ pajānāti.
root_text.an9.45:2.3: Ettāvatāpi kho, āvuso, ubhatobhāgavimutto vutto bhagavatā pariyāyena …pe….
root_text.an9.45:3.1: Puna caparaṁ, āvuso, bhikkhu sabbaso nevasaññānāsaññāyatanaṁ samatikkamma saññāvedayitanirodhaṁ upasampajja viharati, paññāya cassa disvā āsavā parikkhīṇā honti.
root_text.an9.45:3.2: Yathā yathā ca tadāyatanaṁ tathā tathā naṁ kāyena phusitvā viharati, paññāya ca naṁ pajānāti.
root_text.an9.45:3.3: Ettāvatāpi kho, āvuso, ubhatobhāgavimutto vutto bhagavatā nippariyāyenā”ti.

translation_text.an9.45:1.1: “Reverend, they speak of one ‘freed both ways’.
translation_text.an9.45:1.2: What is the one freed both ways that the Buddha spoke of?”
translation_text.an9.45:2.1: “First, take a mendicant who, quite secluded from sensual pleasures … enters and remains in the first absorption.
translation_text.an9.45:2.2: They meditate directly experiencing that dimension in every way. And they understand that with wisdom.
translation_text.an9.45:2.3: To this extent the Buddha spoke of the one freed both ways in a qualified sense. …
translation_text.an9.45:3.1: Furthermore, take a mendicant who, going totally beyond the dimension of neither perception nor non-perception, enters and remains in the cessation of perception and feeling. And, having seen with wisdom, their defilements come to an end.
translation_text.an9.45:3.2: They meditate directly experiencing that dimension in every way. And they understand that with wisdom.
translation_text.an9.45:3.3: To this extent the Buddha spoke of the one freed both ways in a definitive sense.”
